prediction.title: AI Crop Loss Prediction
prediction.subtitle: Predict potential crop losses and get actionable recommendations
prediction.please_wait: Please wait while AI processes the information
prediction.no_data_desc: Fill in the parameters and click Predict Loss to see results
prediction.rec_wheat_1: Apply nitrogen-rich fertilizer at early growth stages
prediction.rec_wheat_2: Ensure proper irrigation during grain filling period
prediction.rec_wheat_3: Monitor for rust and aphid infestation weekly
prediction.rec_wheat_4: Use certified seeds for better germination
prediction.rec_rice_1: Maintain 5cm water level during vegetative stage
prediction.rec_rice_2: Apply potassium fertilizer for disease resistance
prediction.rec_rice_4: Harvest at 25% moisture content for best quality
prediction.rec_maize_1: Ensure adequate nitrogen at knee-height stage
prediction.rec_maize_4: Apply zinc sulfate for better cob development
prediction.rec_mustard_1: Provide adequate irrigation at flowering stage
prediction.rec_mustard_2: Apply sulfur fertilizer for oil content
prediction.rec_sugarcane_3: Control stem borer with biological agents
prediction.rec_sugarcane_4: Remove dry leaves periodically for better growth
prediction.rec_cotton_1: Monitor pink bollworm with pheromone traps
prediction.rec_cotton_4: Pick cotton when bolls are fully opened

disease.title: Plant Disease Detection
disease.subtitle: Upload a photo of your crop to detect diseases instantly

dropzone.title: Upload your crop image
dropzone.subtitle: Drag and drop or click to select an image

result.healthyMessage: Your crop appears healthy! Continue regular care and monitoring.

leafBlight: Yellow to brown spots on leaves

treatment.leafBlight: Apply fungicide containing Mancozeb. Remove and destroy infected leaves. Spray at 10-day intervals.
treatment.powderyMildew: Apply sulfur-based fungicide. Use neem oil spray weekly. Remove severely infected parts.
treatment.rust: Apply fungicide with Propiconazole. Spray at first sign of infection. Repeat every 2 weeks.
treatment.bacterialWilt: Apply copper-based bactericide. Remove and burn infected plants. Solarize soil before planting.

leafBlight: Fungal spores spread by wind and rain
Infected crop debris from previous season
powderyMildew: Dense plant canopy reducing air flow
rust: Prolonged leaf wetness and high humidity
bacterialWilt: Soil-borne bacteria persisting in infected soil
Root injury from transplanting or nematodes

leafBlight: Neem oil spray (5ml per litre of water)
Baking soda solution (1g per litre water)
powderyMildew: Neem oil with soap solution weekly
Milk spray (1 part milk : 10 parts water)
rust: Garlic extract spray (10 cloves per litre)
bacterialWilt: Soil solarization for 4-6 weeks before planting
Copper oxychloride 50% WP (3g per litre water)

expectedRecoveryTime.powderyMildew: 1-2 weeks with regular treatment and monitoring
expectedRecoveryTime.bacterialWilt: 3-5 weeks; severe cases may not fully recover

irrigation.title: Smart Irrigation Advisor
irrigation.subtitle: Optimize water usage with AI-driven irrigation scheduling

recommendation.critical: Soil is very dry. Immediate irrigation required for your crop stage.
recommendation.moderate: Soil moisture is low. Schedule irrigation within 1-2 days.
recommendation.good: Soil moisture is adequate. Maintain current irrigation schedule.

tips.tip1: Use drip irrigation to reduce water usage by up to 40%
tips.tip2: Apply mulch around plants to retain soil moisture and reduce evaporation
tips.tip3: Irrigate in early morning or late evening to minimize water loss
tips.tip4: Collect rainwater for supplemental irrigation during dry spells

schemes.title: Government Schemes Hub
schemes.subtitle: Discover agricultural schemes and subsidies for your farm
schemes.noResults: No schemes found. Try a different search or filter.

pmkisan.name: PM-KISAN
pmkisan.description: Income support of ₹6,000 per year to all landholding farmers

detail.desc: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) is a Central Sector scheme that provides income support of ₹6,000 per year to all landholding farmer families. The amount is transferred in three equal installments of ₹2,000 each directly into the bank accounts of beneficiaries.
detail.eligibility: All landholding farmer families with cultivable land are eligible. The scheme covers small and marginal farmers across India.
detail.benefits: Direct cash transfer of ₹6,000 per year in three installments. No middlemen involved. Aadhaar-based transparent verification system.
detail.howToApply: Visit the official PM-KISAN portal. Submit your Aadhaar number, land documents, and bank account details. You can also apply through your nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or agriculture office.

pmfby.name: PMFBY (Crop Insurance)
pmfby.description: Comprehensive crop insurance covering all stages of farming

detail.desc: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) is a crop insurance scheme that provides comprehensive coverage against crop loss due to natural calamities, pests, and diseases.
detail.eligibility: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas are eligible. Loanee farmers are automatically covered.
detail.benefits: Coverage for all stages of crop cycle. Low premium rates (2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for commercial crops). Timely claim settlement.
detail.howToApply: Contact your nearest bank branch or insurance company. Fill the proposal form and pay the premium share. Loanee farmers are automatically enrolled.

soilhealth.name: Soil Health Card Scheme
soilhealth.description: Soil testing and health card distribution for balanced fertilizer use

detail.desc: The Soil Health Card (SHC) scheme aims to promote balanced fertilizer use based on soil nutrient analysis. Cards are issued every 3 years with crop-specific recommendations.
detail.eligibility: All farmers with agricultural land are eligible for free soil testing.
detail.benefits: Free soil testing every 3 years. Customized fertilizer recommendations for each farm. Improved crop yield and reduced input costs.
detail.howToApply: Visit your nearest agriculture office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra. Provide your land details for soil sample collection by trained officials.

pmksy.name: PMKSY (Watershed)
pmksy.description: Water conservation and watershed management for sustainable farming

detail.desc: Pradhan Mantri Krishi Sinchayee Yojana (PMKSY) focuses on water conservation, watershed management, and promoting efficient irrigation practices.
detail.eligibility: Farmers in watershed project areas are eligible to participate.
detail.benefits: Water harvesting structures. Improved groundwater levels. Reduced soil erosion. Better crop yields through assured irrigation.
detail.howToApply: Apply through your local watershed committee. Participate in village-level planning meetings. Contact the district agriculture department.

kcc.name: Kisan Credit Card
kcc.description: Affordable credit for farmers with flexible repayment options

detail.desc: Kisan Credit Card (KCC) scheme provides short-term loans to farmers for agricultural needs including crop cultivation, post-harvest expenses, and maintenance of farm assets.
detail.eligibility: All farmers, sharecroppers, and tenant farmers are eligible. Landholding farmers and landless agricultural laborers can apply.
detail.benefits: Short-term loans for agriculture needs. Flexible repayment up to 5 years. Personal accident insurance cover. No collateral up to ₹1.6 lakh.
detail.howToApply: Visit any commercial bank, cooperative bank, or regional rural bank. Fill the KCC application form. Submit land records and identity proof.

enam.name: e-NAM
enam.description: National Agriculture Market for transparent price discovery

detail.desc: Electronic National Agriculture Market (e-NAM) is an online trading platform that integrates existing APMC mandis for transparent price discovery and unified market access.
detail.eligibility: All farmers with registered produce can participate. Traders and commission agents with valid licenses can trade.
detail.benefits: Online trading platform for better price discovery. Single license for multiple mandis. Transparent bidding system. Direct payment to farmers.
detail.howToApply: Register at the nearest e-NAM-enabled mandi. Get your biometric ID card. Complete the online registration process to start trading.

upkisanbima.name: UP Kisan Accident Insurance
upkisanbima.description: Accident insurance coverage for farmers in Uttar Pradesh

detail.desc: Uttar Pradesh Kisan Accident Insurance provides financial coverage to farmers in case of accidental death or disability during farming activities.
detail.eligibility: All farmers in Uttar Pradesh between age 18-70 years are eligible.
detail.benefits: Coverage up to ₹5,00,000 for accidental death. Partial disability coverage. No premium payment required by farmers.
detail.howToApply: Contact your local gram panchayat or block development office. Registration is done through the state agriculture department.

mahaasadhar.name: Maharashtra Fasal Sahayya
mahaasadhar.description: Crop assistance scheme for Maharashtra farmers

detail.desc: Maharashtra Fasal Sahayya Yojana provides financial assistance to farmers who suffer crop loss due to natural calamities.
detail.eligibility: Farmers in Maharashtra with crop loss exceeding 33% due to natural calamities are eligible.
detail.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹10,000 per year. Free seed kits for next season. Quick claim processing through online system.
detail.howToApply: Report crop loss to the gram panchayat within 72 hours. Get crop damage assessment done by agriculture officers. Apply through the state portal.

pbkisansathi.name: Punjab Kisan Sathi
pbkisansathi.description: Comprehensive support scheme for Punjab farmers

detail.desc: Punjab Kisan Sathi scheme provides financial assistance and support services to farmers for sustainable agriculture practices.
detail.eligibility: All registered farmers in Punjab are eligible for the scheme benefits.
detail.benefits: Financial assistance of ₹4,000 per acre. Free soil testing. Access to modern farm machinery at subsidized rates.
detail.howToApply: Register at your nearest block agriculture office. Provide land records and Aadhaar card. Complete online registration through Punjab state portal.

about.title: Transforming Indian Agriculture Through Artificial Intelligence
about.description: Sasya (सस्य), inspired by the Sanskrit word for crops and vegetation, combines Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, weather intelligence, and agricultural analytics to help farmers make smarter decisions, improve crop health, and increase productivity sustainably.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text: Empowering every farmer with intelligent technology that simplifies crop management, enables early disease detection, and promotes sustainable farming.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.text: Building the future where every farm benefits from AI-powered insights, precision agriculture, and data-driven decisions.

ai.title: Powered by Artificial Intelligence
ai.text: Sasya uses Machine Learning, Computer Vision, agricultural datasets, and weather intelligence to provide reliable crop insights.

cta.title: Ready to Grow Smarter with Sasya?
cta.text: Join the future of intelligent farming with AI-powered crop monitoring, disease detection, and precision agriculture.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Have questions or feedback? We'd love to hear from you.
